candidates.description: GitHub-style callout/admonition blocks: render > [!NOTE], > [!WARNING], > [!TIP] etc. with colored styling and icons
candidates.rationale: Callout blocks are a GitHub Markdown standard. Mikedown renders them as plain blockquotes. Adding colored callout rendering makes documents more expressive and matches user expectations from GitHub/Obsidian.
candidates.key_insight: Parse blockquotes starting with [!TYPE] via a marked extension or post-processing. Wrap in .callout .callout-{type} divs. Style with CSS using colored left borders and background tints per type (note=blue, warning=yellow, tip=green, danger=red).

candidates.description: Clickable URLs in editor: Cmd/Ctrl+Click on URLs in CodeMirror to open in browser, with underline decoration and hover tooltip
candidates.rationale: When editing markdown, clicking URLs requires switching to preview mode. Adding Cmd+Click to open URLs directly from the editor saves context switches. The underline decoration also improves link visibility in source view.
candidates.key_insight: Create a CodeMirror ViewPlugin that decorates URLs and markdown links with underlines. On mousedown with modifier key, find the URL at cursor position and window.open() it. Add hoverTooltip showing 'Cmd+Click to open link'.

candidates.description: Synchronized editor-preview scrolling: scroll the preview pane proportionally when the editor scrolls in split mode
candidates.rationale: In split mode (editor left, preview right), the editor and preview scroll independently. Synchronizing them so the preview tracks the editor position is a core expectation of markdown editors.
candidates.key_insight: Add a scroll event listener on CodeMirror's scroller element. Compute scroll percentage (scrollTop / scrollable height). Apply the same percentage to the preview's content-wrap element. Debounce with requestAnimationFrame to prevent loops.
